text:
Three-Way Struggle: Part 14 (つの⑭, Mitsu Domoe no Kōbō (Jū Yon)) is the 183 rd chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Summary

Surrounded by the other players with their books open, Gon's group stands with their backs to one another and waits to see what they will do. However, two of the players speak up and assure them that they're there to help, asking the group if they will buy the card from them for 2.5 billion if one of them wins the quiz. They add that the amount isn't unreasonable since it's only 5% of the reward, and Killua realizes that they don't know about the situation with Battera. Killua tells one of the players that they will pay him for the card if he wins, but the player seems dishonest about why he's there, leaving Killua confused. A few others approach Gon and congratulate him, adding that they just traveled to him to watch and asking if they can look at his full book.

Killua guesses that the rest are there to negotiate for the card, and Biscuit agrees with him and asks if they should work together on the quiz like the others are preparing to do. Gon says that they should complete it on their own, with him and Killua betting on which of them will get the better score. Biscuit watches the two and says to herself that both seem so calm especially after the life-or-death fight against the Bombers. She guesses that they enjoyed the game more than any other player, internally wishing them good luck on the quiz while she doesn't participate (since she hates quizzes).

 from Genthru's Countdown]]

Eta begins the event and asks the first question about "Patch of Forest," with Killua already overwhelmed by the five choices. As Eta asks the second question about "Liquor Spring" and the third about "Angel's Breath," the disguised Abengane approaches Genthru still tied up on the ground and touches him on the shoulder. He utters the phrase, "I caught the Bomber" and the Nen beast underneath the disguise vanishes into smoke, leaving Genthru confused as Abengane walks away.

Eta asks about "Paladin's Necklace," and Gon is confident that he answered correctly. She finally reaches the last question, asking what No. 000 is called. As Biscuit sleeps sitting up and Killua struggles to remember the name, Gon's eyes move to "Ruler's Blessing" and he selects it as his answer. Eta says that time is up and prepares to announce the results. Everyone waits with bated breath, and she announces that Gon is the winner with a score of 87 out of 100.

Gon and Killua celebrate, with the other players congratulating him on the achievement. Gon tells Killua that he lost the bet, and as Killua wonders what his score was, an owl suddenly appears in the sky and delivers an envelope to Gon. He opens it and finds the "Ruler's Invitation" card, asking Killua if he has to go to the castle by himself. Killua assures him that they will wait outside, and Biscuit playfully asks which three cards he plans to choose. Just then, all three sense something and then see two players traveling toward them. The Bellam brothers land and tell their informant (the suspicious player Killua was talking with earlier) to leave. Zetsk says that they will bet all their cards on a fight, with the four of them fighting against the two brothers.

Gashta agrees with his brother, but Gon and Killua easily knock the two unconscious. Gon plans to take an "Accompany" card from them, but Killua points out that it won't work since they haven't been to Limeiro before. He proposes using "Drift" since they have probably traveled to every other town in the game. He tells Gon that he will use "Drift" and then come back if he ends up in Limeiro. Killua uses the card and lands outside the town, looking up at the enormous castle. He journeys back and the three use "Accompany" to travel to the town together.

As other players comment on how easily the Bellam brothers were beaten and that Gon's group also defeated the Bomber, Abengane says to himself that while Gon and Killua have improved considerably since they entered the game, they are still weaker than Genthru. After guessing that the group had a great plan to defeat the Bomber, he uses "Magnetic Force" to travel to "Chrollo" and says that Gon's group can have the reward money since he plans to return to the real world and get rich from performing an exorcism. Abengane takes off his disguise and is seen meeting up with Hisoka. In Limeiro, Gon enters the castle and is welcomed by a person waiting for him.
